description: Student submitted a polished narrative that fabricates RR 28 — a vital sign that was never recorded in the findings. Only SpO2 83% and HR 134 are in the evidence record. The prepass identifies the unsupported RR claim in the Assessment section. DMIST is clean. Tests that a fabricated vital in the narrative surfaces as a corroboration violation without penalizing the DMIST, and that the narrative ceiling reflects the unsupported claim.

o2_nrb.label: High-flow O2 via NRB mask (15 LPM)

submitted_docs.dmist: D: 8yo male, dispatched for difficulty breathing. M: History of asthma, 2-hour wheezing onset. I: Asthma exacerbation, no prior episodes, no known ingestion or trauma. S: SpO2 83%, HR 134. T: Transporting to Pediatric ED via ALS intercept.
submitted_docs.narrative: C: Dispatched to 8-year-old male presenting with asthma exacerbation. H: 2-hour onset of wheezing, history of asthma. A: SpO2 83%, HR 134, RR 28. R: O2 via NRB, albuterol SVN, positioned upright. T: ALS intercept, transporting to Pediatric ED.

narrative_unsupported.reason: RR was never recorded in findings — only SpO2 83% and HR 134 documented

critical_actions.description: Administer high-flow O2 via NRB mask

_note: narrative_ceiling=18 because 1 unsupported claim with max_deduction=2 (default). 20 - 2 = 18. All 5 CHART elements are present so no chart-based ceiling applies. Ceiling is prompt-guided (enforce=False) so LLM still adjudicates, but is bounded below.
